perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees not to contact any shareholders, directors, officers, employees, agents, customers, or suppliers of the Company or its affiliates with respect to the Transaction or for the purpose of obtaining information for use in evaluating the Transaction, without the Company’s prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees not to contact any shareholders, directors, officers, employees, agents, customers, or suppliers of the Company with respect to the Transaction or for the purpose of obtaining information for use in evaluating the Transaction, without the Company’s prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: The modification removes 'or its affiliates' from the sentence. This alteration introduces an in-text contradiction as it limits the scope of prohibited contact, while other sections still implicitly consider the affiliates as protected from contact. Thus, the recipient could argue that contact with affiliates is permissible under the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of eighteen (18) months from the date of this Agreement, Recipient will not directly or indirectly (and will not cause or permit any person controlled by Recipient to), solicit for employment, offer to hire, employ, hire, otherwise contract for the services of, or otherwise interfere with the employment relationship of any individual who is an employee of the Company or its affiliates and who is named in the Confidential Information Memorandum furnished by Company (or other similar document) or whom Recipient learns of by name through due diligence efforts provided , however , that this prohibition shall not apply to any person (i) who responds to a general employment advertisement, social media, or whose resume is posted on social media sites, or use of employment agencies, not specifically directed at the Company’s employees, (ii) who has been terminated by the Company prior to commencement of employment discussions with Recipient or its Representatives, (iii) with whom Recipient is currently engaged in employment discussion (as evidenced by written documentation in the event of a dispute), or (iv) who was solicited for employment, offered to hire, employed, hired, or otherwise contracted for the services of the Company with the Company’s prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text prevented the recipient from contacting shareholders, directors, officers, employees, agents, customers, or suppliers of the Company 'or its affiliates'. The changed text removes 'or its affiliates', meaning the recipient is now permitted to contact shareholders, directors, officers, employees, agents, customers, or suppliers of the Company's affiliates. The contradicted text states that the recipient will not solicit or interfere with the employment of any individual who is an employee of the Company 'or its affiliates'. Because the changed text now allows the recipient to contact the affiliates, this introduces a direct contradiction. The change allows contact with affiliates' employees, while the contradicted text restricts solicitation from those same employees.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of eighteen (18) months from the date of this Agreement, Recipient will not directly or indirectly (and will not cause or permit any person controlled by Recipient to), solicit for employment, offer to hire, employ, hire, otherwise contract for the services of, or otherwise interfere with the employment relationship of any individual who is an employee of the Company or its affiliates and who is named in the Confidential Information Memorandum furnished by Company (or other similar document) or whom Recipient learns of by name through due diligence efforts provided , however , that this prohibition shall not apply to any person (i) who responds to a general employment advertisement, social media, or whose resume is posted on social media sites, or use of employment agencies, not specifically directed at the Company’s employees, (ii) who has been terminated by the Company prior to commencement of employment discussions with Recipient or its Representatives, (iii) with whom Recipient is currently engaged in employment discussion (as evidenced by written documentation in the event of a dispute), or (iv) who was solicited for employment, offered to hire, employed, hired, or otherwise contracted for the services of the Company with the Company’s prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of eighteen (18) months from the date of this Agreement, Recipient will not directly or indirectly (and will not cause or permit any person controlled by Recipient to), solicit for employment, offer to hire, employ, hire, otherwise contract for the services of, or otherwise interfere with the employment relationship of any individual who is an employee of the Company and who is named in the Confidential Information Memorandum furnished by Company (or other similar document) or whom Recipient learns of by name through due diligence efforts provided , however , that this prohibition shall not apply to any person (i) who responds to a general employment advertisement, social media, or whose resume is posted on social media sites, or use of employment agencies, not specifically directed at the Company’s employees, (ii) who has been terminated by the Company prior to commencement of employment discussions with Recipient or its Representatives, (iii) with whom Recipient is currently engaged in employment discussion (as evidenced by written documentation in the event of a dispute), or (iv) who was solicited for employment, offered to hire, employed, hired, or otherwise contracted for the services of the Company with the Company’s prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or its affiliates' from this section, it creates an in-text contradiction with the definition of Confidential Information. The Confidential Information is defined to include affiliates, but the non-solicitation clause now excludes affiliates. This raises the question of whether solicitation of affiliate employees using confidential information is a breach of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For all purposes of this Agreement, the term “ Confidential Information ” shall collectively refer to all information or material disclosed or provided by the Company to Recipient, either orally or in writing, or obtained by Recipient from a third party or any other source, regardless of the manner in which it is furnished, concerning any aspect of the business or affairs of the Company or its “ affiliates ” (as such term is defined in Rule 12b-2 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)). Confidential Information also includes any notes, analyses, compilations, data, forecasts, reports, summaries, studies or other material or documents prepared by Recipient which contain, reflect or are based, in whole or in part, on the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'or its affiliates' from the non-solicitation clause, while the 'Confidential Information' definition in the contradicted text explicitly includes information about the Company's affiliates. This creates a direct contradiction because the non-solicitation clause now excludes affiliates, potentially allowing solicitation of affiliate employees using confidential information, which could be argued as a breach under the original definition. This represents a clear and impactful contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall, upon accomplishing the limited purpose of evaluating the Transaction, or at any time upon the request of the Company, immediately destroy or return to the Company all Confidential Information (including notes, writings and other material developed therefrom by Recipient) and all copies thereof and retain none for its files. Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither the Recipient nor its Representatives will be required to erase electronically stored Confidential Information that has been saved to a back-up file or other electronic medium in accordance with its or its Representatives’ ordinary back-up practices. Notwithstanding such return or destruction, Recipient shall continue to be bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall, upon accomplishing the limited purpose of evaluating the Transaction, or at any time upon the request of the Company, immediately destroy or return to the Company all Confidential Information (including notes, writings and other material developed therefrom by Recipient) and all copies thereof and retain none for its files. Notwithstanding such return or destruction, Recipient shall continue to be bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither the Recipient nor its Representatives will be required to erase electronically stored Confidential Information that has been saved to a back-up file or other electronic medium in accordance with its or its Representatives’ ordinary back-up practices' creates an in-text contradiction. The recipient is now required to return or destroy ALL confidential information, but it's practically impossible to ensure all backed-up electronic information is erased, creating conflict and ambiguity and uncertainty over obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall, upon accomplishing the limited purpose of evaluating the Transaction, or at any time upon the request of the Company, immediately destroy or return to the Company all Confidential Information (including notes, writings and other material developed therefrom by Recipient) and all copies thereof and retain none for its files. Notwithstanding such return or destruction, Recipient shall continue to be bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes an exception to the requirement that the recipient must destroy or return all confidential information. The original text acknowledges the practical difficulty of erasing electronically stored information saved to back-up files. By removing this exception, the changed text now requires the impossible, creating a contradiction with the implied understanding that complete erasure may not always be feasible. The recipient must destroy ALL information, but realistically can't for back-ups, thus creating a strong contradiction in obligations as per the explanation. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: In no event shall the Company or its affiliates or any of their respective directors, officers, employees, agents or Representatives (including, without limitation, Guggenheim) have any liability to Recipient relating to or arising out of any use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: In no event shall the Company or any of their respective directors, officers, employees, agents or Representatives (including, without limitation, Guggenheim) have any liability to Recipient relating to or arising out of any use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'or its affiliates' introduces a conflict by potentially exposing the Company's affiliates to liability, while the original text shields them. This contradicts the overall intention of the agreement, which is to protect both the Company and its affiliates. It raises the question of whether the affiliates are indeed protected under the agreement, since they are not explicitly shielded from liability here.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall indemnify and hold harmless the Company and its affiliates and their respective directors, officers, employees, agents and Representatives from and against any and all losses, damages, costs and expenses (including, without limitation, reasonable attorneys’ fees and expenses) caused by or arising out of any breach of this Agreement by Recipient or any breach for which Recipient is responsible hereunder. In any and all actions, suits, proceedings, claims, demands or judgments arising out of or related to this agreement the prevailing party shall be entitled to recovery of attorney’s fees and other costs and expenses.
perturbation.justification: The original text shields both the Company and its affiliates from liability. The changed text removes 'or its affiliates', thus removing the liability protection for affiliates. The contradicted text states that the 'Recipient shall indemnify and hold harmless the Company and its affiliates'. Because the Changed Text does not shield 'affiliates' from liability, while the Contradicted Text requires the recipient to 'hold harmless the company and its affiliates', a direct contradiction exists. This adheres to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall indemnify and hold harmless the Company and its affiliates and their respective directors, officers, employees, agents and Representatives from and against any and all losses, damages, costs and expenses (including, without limitation, reasonable attorneys’ fees and expenses) caused by or arising out of any breach of this Agreement by Recipient or any breach for which Recipient is responsible hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall indemnify and hold harmless the Company and their respective directors, officers, employees, agents and Representatives from and against any and all losses, damages, costs and expenses (including, without limitation, reasonable attorneys’ fees and expenses) caused by or arising out of any breach of this Agreement by Recipient or any breach for which Recipient is responsible hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'and its affiliates' from the indemnification clause creates an in-text contradiction. The recipient is now only obligated to indemnify the Company, but not its affiliates, for losses. However, the confidential information definition still includes information about the affiliates, making their protection ambiguous and creating a conflict of interest regarding indemnification obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For all purposes of this Agreement, the term “ Confidential Information ” shall collectively refer to all information or material disclosed or provided by the Company to Recipient, either orally or in writing, or obtained by Recipient from a third party or any other source, regardless of the manner in which it is furnished, concerning any aspect of the business or affairs of the Company or its “ affiliates ” (as such term is defined in Rule 12b-2 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)). Confidential Information also includes any notes, analyses, compilations, data, forecasts, reports, summaries, studies or other material or documents prepared by Recipient which contain, reflect or are based, in whole or in part, on the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'and its affiliates' from the indemnification clause. The contradicted text explicitly defines 'Confidential Information' as pertaining to both the Company and its affiliates. Therefore, the recipient is now only obligated to indemnify the Company, but not its affiliates, for losses related to confidential information of the affiliates, creating a contradiction.
